patterns.label: Azure DevOps Eval Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: 30 ADO email notifications confirmed again cycle 35 (Task, Requirement, Bug, Feature, Epic prefixes). PM is subscriber/mentioned — not the assignee — in most threads. Structural: 13 cycles of consistent 30/week volume. Machine-parseable subject line patterns, deterministic routing logic. Sub-classifiers confirmed: IcM, Coverage, Evals, Tasks, Epics. Automation ceiling 90–95%. No upward drift — pattern is fully plateaued and mature.

patterns.label: Inbound Eval Strategy and Guidance Q&A (Expert Bottleneck)
patterns.llmRationale: PM remains sole DRI for eval guidance across FDEs, PMs, and Designers. BSI now EMERGENCY (BSI 97): requestTypes=6 (scoping, coverage, metrics, rubric design, timing, eval-driven development), frequency=10/week, persistence=13 cycles, delegationBlocker=1. EvalCon deadline March 20+13 days = April 2 imminent — amplification at peak. 10 new inbound requests this cycle. 35 cumulative. Metrics now span: groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy, multi-turn coherence. Force-multiplier for entire Eval Ecosystem — unblocking this unblocks 7 downstream patterns.

patterns.label: Eval Tooling CLI and Implementation Troubleshooting
patterns.llmRationale: 5 new troubleshooting requests this cycle (CSV import/export, feature flags, preview vs GA, tool_call_accuracy flags). Cumulative 15 across 13 cycles. Pattern stabilized — consistent 5/cycle pace. Deterministic Q&A with known answer set — spawn from eval-coaching-and-scoping. Strong T1 automation candidate. Auto score nudged +1 as answer corpus is now well-characterized after 13 cycles of evidence.

patterns.label: Loop Meeting Notes Creation and Post-Meeting Action Capture
patterns.llmRationale: 8 new occurrences this cycle (4 meeting-side + 4 document-side). PM creates structured Loop notes post-meeting with consistent schema: Agenda, Meeting Notes, Decisions/Open Questions, Follow-up Tasks. Confirmed across both sources for 13 consecutive cycles — fully INSTITUTIONAL. Chain head for meeting-notes ecosystem. SGI 85 HIGH — same structure duplicated across all teams but no canonical source. Camp AIR cascade this cycle amplified downstream note creation.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (Team-Wide Duplication)
patterns.llmRationale: 3 new occurrences this cycle. Multiple people still independently creating eval decks (TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks). Meetings continue flagging version proliferation. SGI 92 CRITICAL — parallelCreators=3, versionCount=5+, explicitly acknowledged in meetings, no canonical source. Camp AIR generated additional variant materials this cycle. EvalCon deadline amplification active — urgency at peak.

patterns.label: External Partner and Customer Eval Sessions
patterns.llmRationale: 6 new sessions this cycle, 13+ hrs again across 50+ external domains (Accenture, HCL, EY, KPMG, Avanade, Cognizant). EDII 5.0+ confirmed for 13 cycles — external intensity is fully structural. Pre-session prep and post-session follow-up remain prime automation targets. Links to parallel-eval-deck-creation SGI gap and eval-coaching bottleneck. Cumulative 39 hrs total across all cycles.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Reporting (Parallel Team Effort)
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly status in done/next/blockers format confirmed again cycle 35. Multiple parallel artifacts: weekly sync updates, bi-weekly newsletters, Cape Day/LT slides. SGI 88 CRITICAL. Template-fill from Loop notes is high automation ceiling target. Pattern is INSTITUTIONAL — 13 cycles unbroken.

patterns.label: Security and Compliance Alert Review
patterns.llmRationale: 7 new alerts this cycle (Purview/Office365: File Blocked, Admin elevation, Loop workspace). Cumulative 21. Machine-generated, classifiable, rule-based. Highest automation ceiling. Single-recipient limits org-wide value — personal productivity win only. Pattern is MATURE and structurally flat.

patterns.label: CAPE and Evals Leadership Strategy Thread Participation
patterns.llmRationale: 4 new occurrences: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive Evals-first Mindset March 18, customer donated evals playbook, EvalCon announcement, multi-reply cross-org thread. Cross-org audience (CAPE, Copilot, leadership, partners). Weekly Evals Sync + Crucible v-Team meetings add cadence layer. Val nudged to 91 — Camp AIR and EvalCon together raise leadership visibility to peak. Digest and brief extraction automatable at ~60% ceiling; judgment required on positioning.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Meeting Portfolio (Calendar Density)
patterns.llmRationale: Six recurring weekly meetings confirmed cycle 35: Evals Sync (Tue), Crucible v-Team (Mon), Manager Team Meeting (Mon), Solution Library Check-In (Mon), Learning & Scaling Fridays, Golden Evals bi-weekly (Thu). Plus 6 weekly 1:1s. MPBI 14. All extended through August — INSTITUTIONAL. Camp AIR adds immersion layer this cycle. Pre-meeting briefing and post-meeting routing are automation surfaces.

patterns.label: Cross-Org Navigation and VOC Routing Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: 4 new routing requests this cycle. PM as informal routing layer between FDEs, MCS Analytics, and PG Eval teams. Codifiable via org chart + DRI mapping + routing rules. Cumulative 12 across 13 cycles. Downstream of eval-coaching bottleneck — resolving that BSI 97 would reduce VOC routing volume proportionally.

patterns.label: EvalCon Content Planning and Positioning Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: 4 new discussions this cycle on CAPE EvalCon presentation: framing, process learnings, submission logistics, leadership alignment. EvalCon deadline now April 2 (13 days from March 20 current date) — Deadline Demand Amplification at peak. Auto nudged to 64, val to 87 reflecting amplified urgency. Brief and submission doc scaffolding automatable. Will likely transition to declining post-April 2.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams (Repos, ZIPs, Specs)
patterns.llmRationale: 8 new artifact shares this cycle: GitHub repos, ZIPs, specs, YAML, Loop components, templates. PM uses Teams as informal distribution hub. Cumulative 24 across 13 cycles. Automation: structured artifact registry + smart-share to relevant channels. Pattern MATURE and stable at 8/cycle pace.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture and MCP/Skills Scoping Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: 6 new discussions this cycle spanning two streams: (1) MCP servers, CLI vs UI, WorkIQ constraints, skill-creator/skill-detector, Copilot Studio declarative agents; (2) URGENT thread on DBS Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites — multiple senior stakeholders, architecture feasibility/risk. Val nudged to 84 — URGENT architectural thread elevates strategic visibility. Architecture FAQ and decision-tree components automatable (T1/T2). High strategic value, moderate automation ceiling.

patterns.label: CAPE Camp AIR Immersive Training and Hackathon Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Camp AIR confirmed cycle 35 with 900 min of immersive sessions (Mon-Tue full days, hackathon standups, EOD debriefs). Immersion Cascade Source: triggers downstream amplification across meeting-notes, status, artifacts, context patterns. Cascade dampening 0.7x applies. Quarterly cadence confirmed. Pre-event prep and post-event debrief scaffolding are automation targets.

patterns.label: Skill-Detector Technical Spec Iterative Build Cycle
patterns.llmRationale: 5 new spec iteration artifacts this cycle (ZIP packages, SKILL.md, scripts, configs). Builder-User Prototyping archetype continues. Scaffold generator for new skill specs, config stubs, and agent state templates. Feeds Skilluminator loop — this is the meta-pattern that generates other patterns. SKILL.md itself is the primary artifact.

patterns.label: Agent Eval Sets Template Maintenance (Recurring Excel Tracker)
patterns.llmRationale: Agent Eval Sets_Template.xlsx confirmed still in active use cycle 35. Living template that evolves with eval needs. Feeds external-partner-eval-sessions pattern. Generate structured eval scenario templates on demand. Connects to partner-eval-enablement-pack-generator as sub-component.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page
patterns.llmRationale: 5 new day-prep files this cycle ([Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day pattern). Recurring morning planning artifact. Single-participant limits org value, but high personal productivity. Calendar digest + email triage + priority summary automation. Connects to meeting-load-triage and ado-notification-triage as upstream data sources.

patterns.label: High-Urgency Escalation Email Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: 1 new URGENT-flagged email this cycle: administrative data correction (URGENT Degree CANNOT be Located to external university). PM escalation tone is distinctive and consistent. Draft generation from structured context is T2 automatable. Pattern confirmed across 13 cycles at low-frequency.

patterns.label: Viva Engage and Community Digest Consumption
patterns.llmRationale: 5 new digest emails cycle 35. Passive consumption, single-participant. Low strategic signal density. Summarization + topic flagging possible but org-wide value minimal. Anti-pattern: Community Noise. Trend declining due to low value ceiling and single-participant scope — not due to absence from signals.

patterns.label: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invite Dispatch
patterns.llmRationale: 2 new outbound Loop workspace invitations cycle 35 (Skilluminator Workspace). Auto-generated by M365 when collaboration initiated. Low time impact but indicates collaboration setup pattern. Cumulative 4 across 2 tracked cycles. Val nudged to 48 — consistent but low-volume pattern. Monitor for growth.

patterns.label: Skilluminator Cycle Dashboard Weekly HTML Refresh
patterns.llmRationale: Dashboard.html with cycle markers confirmed cycle 35 — 2nd cycle observed. PM runs signal ingestion → pattern detection → dashboard refresh → refinement loop. Meta-pattern: the Skilluminator workflow itself is a detectable repeating pattern (Self-Referential Meta-Pattern archetype). Auto nudged to 86 — cycle refresh cadence is highly automatable. Rising as the workflow matures and becomes more established.

patterns.label: 1:1 Stakeholder Check-Ins (Weekly Portfolio)
patterns.llmRationale: 6 weekly 1:1 meetings confirmed cycle 35, 3h 45m total. Relationship-heavy (low automation ceiling for the meeting itself) but pre-meeting prep and talking point generation are automatable at T1. Val nudged to 70 — confirmed structural across 2 tracked cycles. Feeds meeting-load-triage portfolio.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring eval strategy and scoping questions posed to the PM expert bottleneck. Covers: how many eval cases are enough, which metrics to use (groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy, multi-turn coherence), when in the agent lifecycle evals should run, rubric design, and eval-driven development. Routes complex cases to PM (T3) while fully handling FAQ and framework questions (T1). Includes eval-tooling CLI troubleshooting as a sub-module.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases do I need for my Copilot agent?
What metrics should I use to measure groundedness?
When in the agent lifecycle should I start running evals?
Can you walk me through eval-driven development?
My CSV import is failing in the eval CLI — what's wrong?
Is tool_call_accuracy available in preview or GA?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates the #1 expert bottleneck (BSI 97 EMERGENCY — escalated from 78 CRITICAL at cycle 22). PM handles 35 cumulative eval guidance requests across 13 cycles. Deflecting T1 requests frees ~1 hr/week and unblocks the 7-pattern Eval Ecosystem. EvalCon April 2 deadline amplifies urgency to peak.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Generates structured Loop meeting notes from meeting transcript or spoken summary. Output schema: Agenda, Meeting Notes, Decisions/Open Questions, Follow-up Tasks with owners and dates. Integrates with Teams and Loop. Chain head for the largest pattern ecosystem (downstream: weekly-status, action-owner-chasing, transcript-to-loop).
triggerExamples: Create Loop notes for the meeting I just had
Extract action items from this meeting transcript
Generate a structured follow-up doc from these notes
Capture decisions and owners from today's sync
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head for meeting-notes ecosystem. SGI 85 HIGH — every team duplicates this structure. 24 confirmed occurrences across 13 cycles. INSTITUTIONAL pattern. Camp AIR cascade this cycle amplified downstream demand. Standardizing saves estimated 4–6 hrs/week across participants.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies and triages Azure DevOps work-item email notifications by type (Task, Requirement, Bug, Feature, Epic, IcM) and routes them to appropriate attention queues. Suppresses subscriber-only noise, surfaces actionable assignee items, and links to relevant meeting or document context.
triggerExamples: Which ADO notifications need my action today?
Filter my work-item emails — show only what I'm assigned to
Triage this week's ADO notification digest
Route this Bug notification to the right team
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 60 cumulative ADO notifications across 13 cycles at steady 30/week — fully INSTITUTIONAL. PM is subscriber (not assignee) in most. Automation ceiling 93%. Estimated 2–3 hrs/week reclaimed at scale.

candidateSkills.name: eval-enablement-deck-standardizer
candidateSkills.description: Generates or updates a standard eval enablement deck from a canonical template, tailored for specific customer segment, agent type, or partner tier. Eliminates parallel deck creation problem by producing one parameterized output. Addresses SGI 92 CRITICAL condition.
triggerExamples: Create an eval intro deck for this customer
Update the CAPE evals playbook for this partner session
Generate a pitch deck for eval-driven development for an SI partner
Build an EvalCon session deck from our process learnings
candidateSkills.valueProposition: SGI 92 CRITICAL for 13 consecutive cycles. 9 cumulative occurrences, 3 parallel creators, 4.5 hrs in redundant creation. Camp AIR generated additional variants this cycle. EvalCon deadline amplification at peak — April 2 window is final opportunity.

candidateSkills.name: partner-eval-enablement-pack-generator
candidateSkills.description: Assembles a complete partner-facing eval enablement pack for a specific external engagement: eval scenario template, metric guide, timing playbook, and follow-up summary. Parameterized by partner tier (SI, ISV, customer) and agent complexity.
triggerExamples: Prep my eval materials for the Accenture session tomorrow
Generate an eval onboarding pack for a new SI partner
What should I bring to an external eval session with KPMG?
Create a post-session eval summary for ey.com
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 39 cumulative hours in external partner sessions across 50+ domains over 13 cycles. EDII 5.0+ fully structural. Pre-session prep and post-session summaries are prime automation targets. Links to eval-enablement-deck-standardizer as primary artifact source.
